**Mode:** Escalated to ADVERSARIAL after confirming 1 CRITICAL + 3 MAJOR. The token issue is systemic (touches Req1 and Req2), so I hunted adjacent claims and found two additional issues the Architect did not flag (locale-prefix mismatch in AC2.3, and the plan's own internal contradiction on PR count).

**Overall Assessment:** The plan is well-structured, evidence-dense, and correctly honors the mockup-first / Humi-token / TH-EN-parity principles in its prose. But it is built on one factually wrong token (`bg-warning-tint`, which resolves to nothing in this Tailwind v4 build) load-bearing across two requirements, ships a live dead link for 4 PRs in direct violation of its own "no dead ends" DoD, and contains several unfalsifiable ACs. These are fixable in one revision. Not a REJECT — the bones are sound and every defect has a concrete fix.

**Pre-commitment vs. actual:** I predicted the token claim, the dead-link inversion, unfalsifiable ACs, the barrel-export omission, and an Option-A internal contradiction. All five confirmed. I did NOT predict the locale-prefix mismatch in AC2.3 — found it during adversarial sweep.

---

## MUST-FIX (merged with Architect's, deduped)

**MF-1 [CRITICAL] — `bg-warning-tint` does not resolve; replace with `bg-warning-soft` everywhere.** (Confirms Architect BLOCKING #1.)
Independently verified, not taken on faith. The `@theme` block (`globals.css:28-116`) defines `--color-warning-soft: #FEF3C7` (line 58) but **no** `--color-warning-tint`. Contrast line 61: `--color-danger-tint: #FFEDD5; /* alias ... for legacy bg-danger-tint usage — TODO: refactor 28 files */` — the maintainers explicitly aliased `danger-tint` precisely because `-tint` utilities are dead without a matching token. Compiled CSS (`.next/dev/static/chunks/[root-of-the-server]__3b0e865e._.css`) string counts: `warning-tint`=**0**, `warning-soft`=15, `danger-tint`=11. `bg-warning-tint` generates no background.
The plan relies on `bg-warning-tint` in: Req2 ribbon (`login plan L126, AC2.1`), Req1 token table (`L226 .s-pt`, `L228 cov gap`), Req3 (`L267 amber tone`).
- **Fix:** Replace every `bg-warning-tint` with `bg-warning-soft` in the plan AND update the AC2.1 class assertion to `bg-warning-soft`. Correct the cited prior-art reference: `quick-approve-page.tsx:267` uses `bg-warning-tint` too, so it is NOT a valid "reuse this working pattern" precedent — that banner is itself silently broken. Do not cite it as the template.
- Note (out of scope, flag only): ~20 files use the dead `bg-warning-tint`. A separate cleanup ticket is warranted, mirroring the danger-tint TODO.

**MF-2 [MAJOR] — Resolve the wire-before-build dead link; don't mask it with a unit test.** (Confirms Architect BLOCKING #2.)
Verified `src/app/[locale]/roster/` does not exist. PR-1/Req5 repoints sidebar `roster`→`/roster` (`L94, L107`) but `/roster` isn't built until PR-5/Req1 (`L72`). For PRs 1-4 the sidebar has a live 404 link, violating Principle 1 / the mockup DoD "no dead ends." AC5.2 (`L107`) asserts the repoint via unit test, which makes the masking explicit.
- **Fix (pick one, state it):** (a) Move the `roster`/`swap` repoint OUT of PR-1 and into PR-5 so the link goes live the moment the page exists; PR-1 dedupes everything else. OR (b) Land PR-5 (roster) before the repoint. Option (a) is cleaner given the stated sequencing. Either way, remove the dead-link window. Add an AC: "no sidebar leaf resolves to a route with no page.tsx (enumerate routes, assert file exists)."

**MF-3 [MAJOR] — Add `src/components/humi/index.ts` to Req2 file list; deleting ActingBadge breaks the build otherwise.** (Confirms Architect omission #6.)
Verified `index.ts:42`: `export { ActingBadge } from './ActingBadge';`. Importers of the symbol: `Topbar.tsx`, `index.ts`, plus self/`LoginAsRibbon.tsx` (comment only). Physically deleting `ActingBadge.tsx` (plan `L121`) without removing the barrel re-export is a hard TS/build failure.
- **Fix:** Add to Req2 Files: "Remove `ActingBadge` re-export from `src/components/humi/index.ts:42`." Add to AC2.6: "grep `ActingBadge` across `src/` returns zero references (incl. barrel)."

**MF-4 [MAJOR] — Unify the exit route AND fix the locale-prefix bug in AC2.3.** (Extends Architect SHOULD #3.)
Verified: `LoginAsRibbon.tsx:39` → `router.push(\`/${locale}/home\`)`; `PersonaSwitcher.tsx:52` → `router.push(\`/${locale}/admin\`)`. Two different exit destinations for the same conceptual action. Separately, the Architect missed that **both** real implementations are **locale-prefixed**, but plan AC2.3 (`L141`) asserts navigation to bare `/home`. As written, AC2.3 would assert the wrong path and either fail or codify a locale-less route.
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical exit destination (recommend `/${locale}/home` for the ribbon since exiting impersonation returns to the user's own landing; if admins should land on `/admin`, state the rule explicitly). Rewrite AC2.3 and AC4 to assert the **locale-prefixed** path, and assert both components route to the same destination.

**MF-5 [MAJOR] — Make the "no hardcoded hex" / "no red" ACs falsifiable.** (Confirms Architect SHOULD #4.)
AC1.2 (`L246`), AC3.4 (`L275`), AC7.6 (`L307`) say "no raw hex / no `red-*`" but specify no mechanism. A test that doesn't define how it scans is not objectively verifiable and will be silently skipped or faked.
- **Fix:** Specify the exact assertion, e.g.: "Render component, query all elements; assert no `className` token matches `/(^|\s)(bg|text|border)-(red|rose|pink)-\d/` and no inline `style` string matches `/#([0-9a-fA-F]{3,8})\b/`." Apply the same explicit regex to all three ACs. (Reminder for executor: the existing codebase has inline-hex `style` attributes for warning-soft fallbacks — e.g. `admin/employees/page.tsx:51` uses `background: 'var(--color-warning-soft)'` and `[id]/rehire/page.tsx:364` uses `var(--color-warning-soft, #fffbeb)` — so the scan must target only the new component's rendered output, not global.)

**MF-6 [MAJOR] — Resolve the plan's internal contradiction on PR count before execution.** (New — not in Architect findings.)
The plan commits to "Option A (CHOSEN): 7 PRs" (`L361`) as following from the principles, then in the same Decision section (`L373`) says "Architect may collapse PR-2/3/4 into one 'shell chrome' PR if desired." The Architect's recommendation #5 took that bait and recommended exactly the collapse (→5 PRs). So the consensus loop currently has an unresolved fork: is the decision 7 PRs or 5? A plan cannot go to execution with its own PR count undecided.
- **Fix:** The Planner must pick one and state it as the decision, not an option. Given Architect #5, the shared-file argument (Reqs 2/4/6 all edit `Topbar.tsx`/`AppShell.tsx`), and the merge-conflict-minimization driver (`L355`), **collapsing PR-2/3/4 into one `feat/shell-chrome` PR (→5 PRs) is the principle-consistent choice** — it directly serves Decision Driver #1. Update the sequencing diagram, the multi-PR table (`L331-339`), and AC ownership accordingly. If Planner keeps 7 PRs, it must justify why driver #1 doesn't dominate.

---

## Critic mandate checks

| Gate | Verdict | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| **Principle/Option consistency** | **FAIL** | Option A (7 PRs) is stated as chosen but contradicted by its own "may collapse" escape hatch (`L373`) and by Decision Driver #1 (merge-conflict minimization), which actually argues FOR the 5-PR collapse. Unresolved fork (MF-6). |
| **Alternatives depth/fairness** | **Pass** | Options B and C are evaluated with genuine pros AND cons, not strawmanned. C's rejection (cadence + branch-isolation rule) is sound and cites the STA-26 memory rule. B is fairly kept as a live fallback. |
| **Risk mitigation clarity** | **FAIL** | The two blocking risks are NOT concretely mitigated: the token risk is actively wrong (relies on dead `bg-warning-tint`), and the dead-link risk is acknowledged in open-questions but mitigated only by a unit test that masks it rather than a sequencing fix (MF-1, MF-2). |
| **Testable ACs** | **FAIL** | AC1.2/3.4/7.6 unfalsifiable as written (MF-5); AC2.3 asserts a wrong (locale-less) path (MF-4); AC3.3 "no empty hole" is subjective. |
| **Concrete verification steps** | **Mostly Pass** | `npm test -- --run <pattern>`, `npm run build`, i18n-completeness gate, Playwright smoke are all real and runnable. One gap: AC2.1 will assert a class (`bg-warning-tint`) that won't be present after MF-1 fix — keep AC and impl in sync. |
| **Deliberate-mode: pre-mortem** | **FAIL (missing)** | No pre-mortem section exists. For a no-backend mockup the bar is proportional, but there is zero failure-scenario analysis. The two highest-impact failures (token no-op renders invisible amber band; dead-link 404 during HR demo) were both findable by a 3-scenario pre-mortem and both made it into the plan unmitigated. |
| **Deliberate-mode: expanded test plan** | **Partial Pass** | Unit (Vitest) + e2e (Playwright smoke) are well specified per-req. Integration/observability correctly N/A for no-backend mockup. Acceptable proportionally, but see ambiguity in AC falsifiability. |

---

## What's Missing (gap analysis)

- **No pre-mortem.** At minimum 3 scenarios: (1) amber ribbon renders with no background because token is dead → demo looks broken; (2) HR clicks Roster in sidebar during PR-1..4 demo → 404; (3) i18n key added to only one message file → build gate fails mid-PR. The first two are real defects in the current plan.
- **No verification that `--color-accent-alt-soft` / `--color-accent-alt` exist** before Req1 depends on them (`L224, L228`). Open-questions flags it (`open-questions L10`) but it's not gated — and given the `warning-tint` finding, token existence must be VERIFIED, not assumed. (For the record I confirmed `--color-accent-alt: #5B6CE0` and `--color-accent-alt-soft: #E1E4FB` DO exist at `globals.css:45-46`, so those two are fine — but the plan shouldn't rely on luck.)
- **The `.s-night` navy mapping** (`L226`: `bg-[var(--color-ink)]`) puts navy text on navy-ink background unless `text-[var(--color-canvas-soft)]` contrast is verified. No contrast AC.
- **Req7 says "preserve the 861-line component but no longer default" yet doesn't address its existing tests.** Verification (`L310`) hand-waves "ensure old tests either still pass or are intentionally migrated" — that's an unresolved decision, not a plan. Tie to the open-question on replace-vs-flag.
- **No rollback/recovery note** for the Req7 page swap. If `QuickApproveSimple` regresses, is the revert just re-pointing the page import? State it (it's a one-line revert, so this is MINOR — but say so).

## Minor Findings

- AC3.3 "no empty hole" is subjective; reword to "assert Week Recognition card renders in Row 3 right column" (objective).
- Req6 AC6.2 mandates switching TodoBell icon `ListTodo`→`Mail`/`MailOpen` but the icon choice is still listed as "executor + Architect to confirm" (`L155`). An AC must not assert something the plan leaves open. Decide the icon or make the AC conditional.
- Linear coverage: Reqs 1/3/4/6 have no tickets — plan correctly flags this and defers to executor. Acceptable for mockup phase, but the validate-requirement-in-Linear rule means this MUST be closed before each PR. Keep it on the open-questions list (it is).

## NICE-TO-HAVE

- Add the codebase-wide `bg-warning-tint` no-op as a follow-up cleanup ticket (mirrors the `danger-tint` TODO at `globals.css:61`).
- A single shared helper/test asserting "every sidebar href maps to an existing `page.tsx`" would prevent future dead-link regressions beyond just roster.
- Quote the actual blueprint ref (`/tmp/hrms_app_ref.html`) hex→token mapping inline so the executor doesn't re-derive it.

---

## What to change to earn APPROVE next round

1. MF-1: all `bg-warning-tint` → `bg-warning-soft` (plan text + AC2.1 class assertion); drop the `quick-approve-page.tsx:267` "reuse" citation.
2. MF-2: re-sequence so `/roster` link never goes live before the page exists; add a "no dead sidebar route" AC.
3. MF-3: add `index.ts` barrel-export removal to Req2 files + AC2.6.
4. MF-4: one canonical, **locale-prefixed** exit route asserted in both AC2.3 and AC4.
5. MF-5: explicit regex-based hex/red scan defined in AC1.2/3.4/7.6.
6. MF-6: resolve 7-PR-vs-5-PR fork — adopt the 5-PR shell-chrome collapse (principle-consistent) and update sequencing + PR table, or justify keeping 7.
7. Add a proportional pre-mortem (≥3 scenarios) and confirm each is mitigated in the plan.

Land those seven and this is an APPROVE. The plan's research depth and principle articulation are genuinely strong — the failures are concrete and mechanical, not structural.

**Open Questions (unscored):**
- Did the planner intend `bg-warning-tint` because the blueprint ref literally uses it, or was it copied from the (broken) `DelegationBanner`? Either way the fix is the same, but it affects whether other ref-derived tokens are trustworthy.
- Should exiting impersonation as an admin land on `/admin` (work context) or `/home` (personal)? Product call, not a code fact — needs the planner/stakeholder to decide before AC2.3 is finalized.

**Relevant files (absolute):**
- `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/app/globals.css` (token SSOT; warning-tint absent at `@theme` 28-116, danger-tint alias at 61)
- `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/humi/index.ts:42` (ActingBadge barrel re-export)
- `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/humi/shell/LoginAsRibbon.tsx:39` (exit→`/${locale}/home`)
- `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/humi/shell/PersonaSwitcher.tsx:52` (exit→`/${locale}/admin`)
- `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/humi/TodoBell.tsx` (uses `ListTodo`, footer `/${locale}/ess/workflows`)
- `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/messages/{en,th}.json` (i18n SSOT, parity-gated)

The barrel export is exactly at `index.ts:42` as stated; ActingBadge has precisely the references the plan names (barrel, Topbar import L31, Topbar JSX L186, plus the LoginAsRibbon comment L9 it correctly excludes); every leaf target route except `/roster` has a real `page.tsx`, confirming the route-existence gate is runnable and that `/roster` is correctly absent in PR-1.

## Summary

I re-read all 447 lines of the revised plan and verified the load-bearing claims against source. **All 7 MUST-FIX items are genuinely resolved in the file**, not just asserted. The 5-PR collapse did not introduce a too-large PR, the route-existence gate is runnable (confirmed: 25/26 leaf paths have real `page.tsx`, only `/roster` is absent — exactly as PR-1 expects), and AC numbering is consistent. **Verdict: APPROVE.** Two minor non-blocking notes for the Critic/executor below.

## MUST-FIX verification (each checked against the file + source)

| MF | Claim | Verified | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|

Every load-bearing amber now reads `bg-warning-soft`: ribbon L141/AC2.1 L154, `.s-pt` L242, coverage-gap L245/AC1.3 L264, home tiles L286/AC3.4 L294. The only `bg-warning-tint` mentions remaining are **intentional forbidden-token callouts** (L62, L66, L141, L242, L264, L286, L409, L445). DelegationBanner is now cited as broken prior-art to avoid (L66, L141), not to copy. No load-bearing usage remains. |

Confirmed runnable: all PR-1 leaf targets resolve to real `page.tsx`, `/roster` is the only MISS — so AC5.2 passes in PR-1 (roster still →`/manager-dashboard`, which exists) and again in PR-3 (now →`/roster`, which exists). No window. |

AC2.6 L159 asserts barrel-free + Topbar-free; build-fail proof noted L161. The plan's ref-count is exactly right: barrel + Topbar import + Topbar JSX + the one comment it correctly excludes. |

## Did the revision introduce new problems? (the three risks you flagged)

1. **5-PR collapse → too-large PR-2?** No. PR-2 is: 1 dropdown→modal, 2 dead-code activations (ribbon + TodoBell import), 1 icon swap, 1 component delete + barrel edit, 1 new pure helper (`persona-tiers.ts`). Fixed internal commit order (L360) keeps it reviewable. This is the correct granularity; the accepted-cost is honestly recorded in the ADR (L442).
2. **Route-existence gate unrunnable?** No — confirmed runnable. 25/26 leaf paths map cleanly to `src/app/[locale]/<path>/page.tsx`. One subtlety the executor must handle (note below).

**"Cut outright" optimizes for a clean screenshot and sacrifices the thing an HR sign-off demo is actually for: confirming the system's *scope*.** This is a UI mockup whose entire purpose (per CLAUDE.md) is HR confirming *direction* before real build. Option B's "disabled/coming-soon" rows — which the plan dismisses as clutter — are the conventional pattern *for exactly this scenario*: they communicate roadmap without faking functionality. **Cleanest-menu vs. blueprint-parity — and the test is literally named `sf-parity-sidebar`.** The plan rewrites the *parity* test to assert a *reduced, non-parity* IA. That's not wrong, but it's a quiet redefinition: the test's name and its top-comment ("port ตาม blueprint เป๊ะ" lineage, "9 ESS leaves") encode a contract that the menu *mirrors* the Blueprint/SF structure. After this plan, the test asserts a deliberately *divergent* 25-leaf menu while still being called "sf-parity." Future readers will see a passing "parity" test and assume the menu matches the Blueprint — when it intentionally doesn't. ---

# Code Review Report — VERIFY stage, `feat/sidebar-ia-restructure`

**Scope:** SHELL + ROSTER + LEAF lanes only (per brief). Pre-existing benefits/hrbp/workflow-api defects ignored.
**Method:** baseline diff vs `2884a07e`, full read of every changed/new source file, token-guardrail grep across all 19 touched files, falsifiable-scan inspection, `tsc --noEmit`, and the full team test suite.

(Note: LSP server `typescript-language-server` is not installed in this env, so I substituted `tsc`.)

## Priority questions — answered

**1. sf-parity-sidebar rewrite — PRESERVES intent, not weakened. LEGITIMATE.**
The rewrite migrates from the old accordion API (`button` + `aria-expanded`) to the new rail/panel API (`role="tab"` + `aria-selected`) — a genuine UI mechanic change, mirrored 1:1 in `Sidebar.tsx` (rail tabs at lines 298-313, `aria-selected` 302, `.locked`/`disabled` 303-305). The SF-parity *intent* is preserved or strengthened:
- Leaf set: still asserts all 9 ESS workspace leaves + exact hrefs (test L179-207), team/hr/system leaves with hrefs (L235-274).
- Group structure: all 4 triggers always render (L137-143); locked-group semantics preserved as disabled tab + `.locked` class (L159-169) — equivalent to the old "—" count assertion.
- Persona gating: still asserts employee→workspace-only, manager→team, hr_admin→team+hr, hr_manager→system.
- **Two assertions changed, both legitimately reflecting real Req5 product changes verified against `Sidebar.tsx`:**
  - hr_admin **no longer** unlocks system (test L250-261). TRUE to code: system leaves `roles`/`integrations` both `show:['sysadmin']`, and `sysadmin`→`hr_manager` (Sidebar L176-177, 193), so `hr_admin` cannot reach them. The old "hr_admin unlocks system" assertion was tied to decoration leaves that were genuinely cut. **Not a coverage loss** — coverage actually tightened (now asserts system *stays locked*).
  - `อนุมัติ` → `กล่องงาน · อนุมัติ` merge (test L241-245, asserts merged leaf present AND old leaf absent). TRUE to code: Sidebar L123 merged the two duplicate `/quick-approve` leaves per the unified-inbox rule. This is a *stronger* assertion than before.
  No parity assertion was softened to merely turn green.

**2. Token guardrails (MF-1) — CLEAN across all 19 touched files.**
- (a) `bg-warning-tint` dead class: **0 occurrences.** Amber consistently uses `bg-warning-soft` (LoginAsRibbon:64, RosterGantt:25, QuickActionsTile:57, CoverageStrip).
- (b) Hardcoded hex in components: **0 occurrences.**
- (c) `red-/rose-/pink-` Tailwind classes: **0 occurrences.**
- (d) Danger: correctly `bg-danger`/`text-danger`/`var(--color-danger)` (pumpkin) — TodoBell:58, quick-approve-simple:200-201. No red.

**3. Falsifiable scans — all REAL, none stubbed.** Each renders the component and iterates actual DOM:
- AC1.2 `RosterGantt.test.tsx:58-70` — `container.querySelectorAll('*').forEach(...)` asserting className `not.toMatch(RED_CLASS)` and style `not.toMatch(HEX)`.
- AC1.3 `CoverageStrip.test.tsx:70-75` — same pattern over all descendants.
- AC3.4 `QuickActionsTile.test.tsx:97-112` — iterates `[class]` and `[style]` elements, asserts no red/rose/pink, no hex.
- AC7.6 `quick-approve-simple.test.tsx:156-179` — same, plus a dedicated Reject-button check.

**4. Dead-code cleanup — COMPLETE.** `ActingBadge.tsx` deleted; import removed from `Topbar.tsx`; barrel export removed from `index.ts`; only a documentation comment remains in `LoginAsRibbon.tsx:9` (plus the deletion-asserting tests). i18n: the `acting_badge` namespace **is now orphaned** (`messages/en.json:4848`, `th.json:4771`) — no `useTranslations('acting_badge')` reference anywhere in `src/`. Flagged LOW (see below).

**5. Humi conformance — STRONG.** New surfaces use `@/components/humi` primitives: roster page (Card/CardEyebrow/Button/EmptyState), roster modals (Modal/Button), quick-approve-simple (Card/Button/DataTable). Token utilities throughout (`bg-canvas-soft`, `text-ink`, `border-hairline`, `rounded-[var(--radius-md)]`, `var(--color-*)`). Cream-canvas + navy-ink baseline, teal/accent for primary. No legacy non-Humi card classes, no stark white/black admin styling.

**6. MF-4 exit-route convergence — CONFIRMED.** Both exit to `/${locale}/home`: `LoginAsRibbon.tsx:55-58` (`handleExit`) and `PersonaSwitcher.tsx:54-59` (`handleExit`). Neither targets `/admin`.

## Findings

**LOW — Orphaned `acting_badge` i18n namespace** (`src/frontend/messages/en.json:4848`, `th.json:4771`)
After the ActingBadge deletion, this namespace (`actingAs`, `clickToExit`) has no consumer. Harmless dead i18n. **I did NOT fix it** — `messages/{en,th}.json` are outside the declared lane scope and the diff shows other teams' additions in those files; editing them risks collateral changes. Recommend the SHELL lane owner remove these two blocks in a follow-up.

**NIT — Invalid `col-span-[24]` arbitrary class** (`src/frontend/src/components/roster/RosterGantt.tsx:93`)
Not a generated Tailwind utility; a dead no-op. The adjacent inline `style={{ gridColumn: '2 / span 24' }}` (L94) does the actual positioning, so zero functional impact. Could be dropped for cleanliness.

**NIT — Redundant/contradictory className on Reject button** (`src/frontend/src/components/manager/quick-approve-simple.tsx:200-201`)
`className="bg-danger text-danger"` (solid danger bg + danger text = self-cancelling) is fully overridden by the inline `style` (`danger-soft` bg + `danger` text). Renders correctly because inline wins; the className is just sloppy. Token choice is correct (pumpkin danger, not red).

**NIT — Token-scan regex requires numeric suffix** (`RED_CLASS = /…-(red|rose|pink)-\d/` in all four scan tests)
Would not catch a bare `bg-red`/`text-rose` (no number). Theoretical only — my independent grep confirmed no bare red/rose/pink tokens exist in the components. Tightening the regex would future-proof the guard.

**Pre-existing (NOT team-introduced, out of scope to fix) — QuickActionsTile hardcoded `/th/` hrefs**
`DEFAULT_ESS_ACTIONS` hrefs are hardcoded `/th/...` and `home/page.tsx:285` does not pass `locale` to `<QuickActionsTile>`, so EN-locale users get TH-route links. Verified present at baseline `2884a07e`; the team actually *added* a `locale` prop to the component (an improvement). Acceptable for a TH-primary mockup. Worth a future ticket.

## Positive observations
- The sf-parity rewrite is honest engineering: every changed assertion traces to a real, verifiable code change in `Sidebar.tsx`, and coverage tightened (asserts system *stays* locked, asserts old approvals leaf *absent*).
- Excellent self-documenting comments explaining each Req5 dedupe/cut decision inline at the MODULES definitions.
- `persona-tiers.ts` is clean: Set-based dedup, canonical `TIER_ORDER` rendering, `tierChips` alias matching the task's API name.
- ActingBadge removal is fully wired (file + import + barrel + tests asserting all three).
- Falsifiable token scans are a genuinely good defense-in-depth pattern that walks rendered DOM rather than asserting on source strings.

## Verdict

**APPROVE-WITH-NITS** (team lanes only)

No BLOCKER or HIGH issues. All six priority questions resolve favorably with file:line evidence. Zero in-lane type errors, 142/142 tests pass, token guardrails clean, dead code removed, MF-4 convergence confirmed, Humi conformance strong. The four findings are one LOW (orphaned i18n, out-of-lane so intentionally not fixed by me) and three NITs (a dead Tailwind class, a sloppy-but-correct className, and a regex-tightness suggestion) — none block sign-off.

**I made no code changes.** The only candidate quick-fix (orphaned `acting_badge` i18n) lives in out-of-lane files that carry other teams' uncommitted edits, so per the brief's guardrail ("do NOT touch pre-existing … code") I left it for the lane owner and flagged it instead.

Key files reviewed: `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/humi/shell/Sidebar.tsx`, `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/__tests__/sf-parity-sidebar.test.tsx`, `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/humi/shell/{LoginAsRibbon,PersonaSwitcher,Topbar,AppShell}.tsx`, `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/humi/{TodoBell,QuickActionsTile,index.ts}`, `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/lib/persona-tiers.ts`, `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/app/[locale]/{roster,home}/page.tsx`, `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/roster/{RosterGantt,CoverageStrip,ShiftEditorDrawer,ShiftSwapModal,BulkAssignModal}.tsx`, `/Users/tachongrak/Projects/hr/src/frontend/src/components/manager/quick-approve-simple.tsx`.
